title: Lowered Through the Roof
subtitle: The paralytic let down through the opened roof, and forgiven before he was healed

stillness: Be still, and come into the house where He is teaching.
The inward word is the thought of the mind.
Let Him read what you would not dare to say aloud.
gospel: Jesus stepped into the boat, crossed over, and came into His own city.
And behold, while He was teaching the crowds in the house,
four men came carrying a paralytic on a bed.
Finding the door blocked by the multitude, unable to enter,
they climbed onto the roof, uncovered it,
and through the tiles let him down, bed and all, into the midst, before Jesus.
And seeing their faith, Jesus said:
Take heart, son; your sins are forgiven you.
And behold, certain of the scribes said within themselves: This man blasphemes.
But Jesus, seeing their thoughts, said:
Why do you think evil in your hearts?
Which is easier, to say, Your sins are forgiven, or to say, Rise and walk?
But that you may know the Son of Man has power on earth to forgive sins,
then He said to the paralytic: Rise, take up your bed, and go to your house.
And he rose, and went away to his house, glorifying God.
And the crowds, seeing it, were afraid, and glorified God,
who had given such power to men.
behold: Behold the Lord Jesus crossing the water in a little boat,
He who could have gone over on foot,
for He did not always wish to show His power in wonders, lest He harm the mystery of His Incarnation.
He teaches us to use the boat and the help of penance upon the sea of this world,
that we may come at last into the heavenly city.
Behold the four men, blocked at the door by the crowd,
climbing the roof, tearing it open, lowering their friend through the tiles.
This was the great sign of their faith.
for he would not have let himself be carried and lowered had he not trusted to be healed.
And see how, before He touches the body, the Lord heals the soul.
consider: See the wonder of His humility.
The despised, the feeble, the man wholly loosened in every joint of his limbs,
whom the priests did not think worthy to touch,
this one He calls: son.
He does not say, I forgive, but, your sins are forgiven,
that He might breathe into us humility and tenderness.
The scribes did not dare to speak aloud for fear of the people,
so they murmured within;
yet the inward word is the thought of the mind, and He saw it.
By what power I see your thoughts, by that same power I can forgive sins,
for thoughts are the spring of good and the wellspring of evil.
So when you feel an evil thought beating at you,
at once turn upon yourself and let Christ's own word be spoken to your heart:
Why do you think evil in your hearts?
And see: the man rose, took up the bed that had been the proof of his sickness,
that it might now become the proof of his health,
and walked home, needing no one to carry him.
Great is that power, where without any delay healing follows the command,
so that no one could doubt He had forgiven the paralytic his sins,
He who bade the same man take up his pallet and walk.
prayer: who in Your Passion climbed into the little boat of the Cross,
in Your Resurrection crossed the water,
and in Your Ascension came into Your own city:
behold, the fear of my sins,
of the danger of approaching weakness,
and of the uncertainty of death,
offers to You a soul loosened by the sickness of sin.
Speak to the one who lies silent in his sins,
that he may trust in the grace of forgiveness,
rise again through contrition and confession,
take up his bed through making amends,
and walk by the growth of the virtues into his home, eternal blessedness;
that the crowds of the faithful may see, and fear, and glorify God,
who has given such power for the good of men.

latinJewel.latin: Magna virtus, ubi sine mora salus imperium comitatur.
latinJewel.english: Great is that power, where healing follows the command without any delay.
